State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 20, 1977

77-03-200

Senator Robert P. Nimmo
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Senator Nimmo:

Thank you for your letter of May 11, 1977, asking for my
comment on the letter you received from San Luis Obispo County
District Attorney Robert N. Tait.

Mr. Tait criticizes Elections Code Section 29410 because
he interprets the section to punish anonymous posters, etc.,
only when the poster is paid for. Under Mr. Tait's interpretation, all printed material must contain the name and address
of the printer and publisher, but no penal sanction is applicable when an individual himself reproduces or prints material
in violation of the section.

Although the somewhat related provisions of Government
Code Section 84305 are within the Political Reform Act, Section
29410 does not come under the jurisdiction of the Fair Political
Practices Commission, and I can claim no specific expertise in
interpreting the section. Nor have I done any research on the
question Mr. Tait raises. Nevertheless, I do not believe his
interpretation is necessarily correct. The last sentence of
Section 29410 seems to make any violation of the section a misdemeanor. This would include a violation of the first paragraph,
which prohibits the anonymous printed material. The prohibition
of a payment for such anonymous material, contained in the first
sentence of the second paragraph, can thus be interpreted as an
additional remedy to make sure that the first paragraph is not
violated.

Although Mr. Tait's interpretation is not necessarily correct, he does raise an interesting point, and clarification by
the Legislature may be appropriate. I understand that Assembly
Bill 1375 would make changes in Section 29410, and you might want
to look at that bill in the context of Mr. Tait's letter.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has taken no position on AB 1375. We have had some discussions in the past on

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5661 · EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5601 · ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6941

Senator Robert P. Nimmo
May 20, 1977
Page Two


the merits of Section 29410 as a whole. During these discussions strong support and strong opposition to that section have been expressed by differing members of the Commission and members of the staff. Personally, I have no strong feelings on the subject.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

California State Senate

WATER RESOURCES
ELECTIONS AND

JOINT COMMITTEE ON FAIRS ALLOCATION
AND CLASSIFICATION

ROBERT P. NIMMO
SENATOR

SANTA CRUZ, MONTEREY, SAN LUIS OBISPO COUNTIES,
AND A PORTION OF SANTA BARBARA COUNTY

May 11, 1977

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

Enclosed please find correspondence from Robert N.
Tait, District Attorney of San Luis Obispo County, regarding
Election Code Section 29410.

I would appreciate it if you could comment on
District Attorney Tate's letter.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

ROBERT P. NIMMO

RPN:ctd

Encls.

ROBERT N. TAIT

District Attorney
SAN LUIS OBISPO COUNTY

April 4, 1977


Hon. Robert Nimmo, Senator
California State Legislature
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Bob:

Election Code Section 29410 presently provides that:

"Every bill, placard, poster, pamphlet, or other printed
matter having reference to an election or to any candi-
date shall bear upon its face the name and address of
the printer and publisher.

No payment, therefore, shall be made or allowed unless the
name and address is so printed.  A violation of this Section
shall constitute a misdemeanor."

As an officer charged with the responsibility of prosecuting
violations of the State election laws, we must admit to con-
fusion as to the precise intention of the Legislature in
adopting the language of Election Code Section 29410.
Although the section proscribes unidentified printed election
materials, it apparently only punishes that activity if the
printed matter is paid for.  In those cases where an individual
reproduces or prints unidentified election matter at his own
expense, the Penal provision of Section 29410 is apparently
inapplicable.  To our way of thinking this anomalous result
makes little or no sense, and is possibly violative of the
equal protection clauses of both the Federal and State constitu-
tions.

Notwithstanding our opinion on the matter, if our interpre-
tation of the legislation is correct we believe that revision
is necessary to clarify this law and enable this office to
properly fulfill its mandated duty of enforcing the law.


PLEASE REPLY TO:

☒ COURTHOUSE ANNEX
  SAN LUIS OBISPO, CA 93401
  TELEPHONE: 805-543-3364

☐ FAMILY SUPPORT DIVISION
  1010 PALM STREET
  SAN LUIS OBISPO, CA 93401
  TELEPHONE: 805-544-8200

☐ PASO ROBLES BRANCH OFFICE
  573 12th STREET
  PASO ROBLES, CA 93446
  TELEPHONE: 805-238-3846

April 4, 1977
Page 2


Thank you for your attention to this ma
any further assistance, please so infor

Very truly yours,

[signature]
ROBERT N. TAIT
District Attorney
